_note: Cross-domain web-quality checkpoints (performance, discoverability, forms). Reusable by content, marketing, portfolio, and blog archetypes — anything public-facing.

checkpoints.title: Page performance meets Core Web Vitals
checkpoints.why: On content and marketing sites, slow loads directly cost users and SEO ranking. The common AI-generated tells - unsized/uncompressed images, render-blocking scripts, no lazy-loading - are measurable against documented Core Web Vitals thresholds.

levels.3: Large unoptimized images, render-blocking scripts, no lazy-loading; LCP well over 2.5s on mobile.
levels.6: Images sized/compressed and lazy-loaded below the fold; scripts deferred/async; LCP < 2.5s, CLS < 0.1 on mobile.
levels.9: Performance budget enforced; responsive images, preloaded critical assets, minimal blocking; consistently green Core Web Vitals.

verify.reasoning: Are images sized, compressed, and lazy-loaded? Are scripts deferred/async or render-blocking? Would the largest contentful paint plausibly land under 2.5s on mobile? Is there any performance budget?

assertions.check: Images have explicit dimensions and below-the-fold images are lazy-loaded.
Scripts are async/deferred, not render-blocking in the head.
Core Web Vitals (LCP/CLS/INP) measured against documented thresholds.

checkpoints.title: Discoverable: titles, meta, and semantic structure
checkpoints.why: A site nobody can find or preview correctly underperforms regardless of quality. Per-page titles, meta descriptions, Open Graph/Twitter cards, semantic landmarks, and a sitemap are documented, mechanical wins AI scaffolds routinely skip.

levels.3: Generic or missing per-page title / meta description; no Open Graph; non-semantic div soup; no sitemap.
levels.6: Unique title + meta description per page; Open Graph/Twitter cards; semantic landmarks (header/nav/main); sitemap + robots.
levels.9: Structured data (schema.org) where relevant, canonical URLs, per-page social previews verified, clean crawlable routing.

verify.reasoning: Does each page have a unique title and meta description? Are Open Graph / social-preview tags present? Is the markup semantic (landmarks, headings in order)? Is there a sitemap and robots?

assertions.check: Each page sets a unique title and meta description.
Open Graph / social-card tags are present.
Semantic landmark elements are used (header, nav, main, footer).

checkpoints.title: Forms validate server-side and resist spam/abuse
checkpoints.why: A contact or lead form that trusts the client and has no spam protection becomes a spam relay and a source of bad data. Server-side validation plus a honeypot/CAPTCHA and rate limiting are the established floor; exact thresholds are reasoned.

levels.3: Client-side validation only; no spam protection; submissions trusted and emailed/stored as-is.
levels.6: Server-side validation of every field; a honeypot or CAPTCHA; rate limiting; output escaped/sanitized.
levels.9: Layered: server validation, bot mitigation, rate + dedup limits, clear error/success states, and abuse monitoring.

verify.reasoning: Is form input validated on the server, not just the client? Is there any spam/bot mitigation (honeypot, CAPTCHA, rate limit)? Is submitted content escaped/sanitized before storage or email?

assertions.check: Form fields are validated server-side, not only in the browser.
Spam/abuse mitigation (honeypot, CAPTCHA, or rate limit) is present on submit.
